sources.title: Gangster from Chicago 1950 Film-Noir | They Hi-jack $40 Million in merch from trucks!
sources.description:
Trucker hits the highway to clear his name - but the road to redemption is paved with double-crosses and stolen cargo!
When Joe stops to help stranded motorists, he's knocked out and his rig vanishes - making him the prime suspect in a corporate hijacking ring. With cops on his tail and a treacherous executive framing him, Joe must outdrive roadblocks and outsmart backstabbers before his parole runs out.

Original Title: Hi-Jacked (1950) | AKA: Gangster from Chicago

Black & White version: https://youtu.be/XVKbW_MFz3k


⭐️ STARRING
Jim Davis (Joe Harper)
Marcia Mae Jones (Jean Harper)
Sid Melton ('Killer')
David Bruce (Matt)
Paul Cavanagh (Hagen)
Ralph Sanford (Stephen Clark)
House Peters Jr. (Hank)
Iris Adrian (Aggie)

🎬 CREW CREDITS
Directed by Sam Newfield (The Mad Monster, Dead Men Walk)
Written by Orville H. Hampton & Fred Myton
Poverty Row grit by prolific producer Sigmund Neufeld (I Accuse My Parents)
Hard-hitting cinematography by Philip Tannura (Flame of Stamboul,  A Night of Terror)

🎯 WHY WATCH?
A nitro-fueled B-noir where every mile counts and trust is the deadliest cargo!
☁️ Jim Davis pre-Dallas glory as the fist-swinging trucker fighting corporate crooks!
💥 Sid Melton's scene-stealing goon delivers laughs amid the highway chaos!
☕️ Iris Adrian's diner slang - a masterclass in 1950s sass that'll make you order black coffee!

🔔 SUBSCRIBE @CultCinemaClassics

Film Noir • Crime • Drama • Action • Vintage Thrillers • Cult Classics • 1950s Cinema

sources.title: Hi-Jacked (1950, film noir) EXPOSED... Secret operations of the nation's motorized mobsters!
sources.description:
Ex-con trucker shifts gears from suspect to sleuth!
A truck driver stops on a rainy road at night to help a stranded motorist, but it turns out to be a ruse--he is attacked, knocked out and his truck stolen. Since he has a criminal record, the police immediately suspect he's involved in the hijacking, and their suspicions are reinforced later when he is discovered--unknown to him--to be hauling stolen merchandise. He realizes he is being set up as a fall guy by the organization behind the truck hijackings, and he and a friend set out to determine who is trying to set him up, and why.

Colorized version: https://youtu.be/vFP9T_ksoAQ

Alternative title: Gangster from Chicago (1950)

Director: Sam Newfield
Writers: Orville H. Hampton, Fred Myton, Raymond L. Schrock
Stars: Jim Davis, Marcia Mae Jones, Sid Melton

Genres: Crime, Drama, Film-Noir
Release date: July 7, 1950
Filming location: Gorman, California, USA

https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=UUMOycDFnpMeWzaITQSD1dWsOA

@CultCinemaClassics
